'''Daniel Smith''' (born 20 March 1993) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} and {{rlp|lf}} for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

Smith is a [[Leeds Rhinos]] academy graduate. He previously played for [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], and [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. He has spent time at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Featherstone Rovers]] and [[Halifax Panthers]] on dual registration from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] and [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] respectively. He has also spent time at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], and at [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] on loan from both [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] and [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].

==Background==
Smith was born in [[Pontefract]], West Yorkshire, England. He is the brother of fellow rugby league footballer [[Cameron Smith (rugby league born 1998)|Cameron Smith]].<ref name="Leeds Rhinos: Memorable night if not result admits débutant Smith">{{cite web|url=http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos/leeds-rhinos-memorable-night-if-not-result-admits-debutant-smith-1-7887108|title=Leeds Rhinos: Memorable night if not result admits débutant Smith|publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post|date=2016-05-02|access-date=2016-09-13}}</ref> He attended [[Castleford High School Technology and Sports College|Castleford High School]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-10-11 |title=Super League: Wakefield Wildcats land former Leeds Rhino Daniel Smith |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/8968065/super-league-wakefield-wildcats-land-former-leeds-rhino-daniel-smith |access-date=2023-05-23 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

Smith played at junior level for amateur side [[Castleford Lock Lane]]. In 2009, at the age of 15, he joined the academy at the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name="oldham">{{cite web|title=Oldham snap up Rhinos prospect|url=http://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/11/roughyeds/70773/oldham-snap-up-rhinos-prospect|publisher=Chronicle Online|access-date=23 February 2016|date=19 June 2012}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2009/02/04/rhinos-sign-latest-young-stars/|title=Rhinos sign latest young stars|last=Daly|first=Phil|date=2009-02-04|website=Leeds Rhinos|language=en|access-date=2020-04-22}}</ref>

==Career==
=== Leeds Rhinos ===
Smith was promoted to the senior [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] squad prior to the [[2012 Leeds Rhinos season|2012 season]], and was allocated squad number 30.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-announce-2012-squad-list/|title=Leeds announce 2012 squad list|date=2011-11-10|website=Love Rugby League|access-date=2020-04-22}}</ref> However, he did not make any first-team appearances for the Rhinos.

==== Oldham (dual registration) ====
In June 2012, Smith joined [[Championship 1]] side [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] on dual registration.<ref name="oldham" /> He made four appearances for the Roughyeds.

=== South Sydney Rabbitohs ===
Smith moved to Australia in 2013 to play for [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] under-20 side, where he made 24 appearances.<ref name="huddersfield">{{cite web|title=Huddersfield Giants prop Daniel Smith setting sights on first-team shirt for Super League campaign|url=http://www.examiner.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-prop-daniel-smith-10574904|website=Huddersfield Examiner|access-date=23 February 2016|date=10 December 2015}}</ref> At the end of his time down under, he recalled "Australia was the best experience of my life. It’s changed me massively on and off the field."<ref name="wakefield lrl">{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-sign-prop-smith/|title=Wakefield sign prop Smith|last=Gordon|first=James|date=2013-10-11|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-22}}</ref>

=== Wakefield Trinity Wildcats ===
Smith returned to England at the end of the year, signing a two-year contract with the [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in October 2013.<ref name="wakefield lrl" /><ref>{{cite web|last1=Rigg|first1=Nick|title=Wakefield Wildcats: South Sydney Rabbitohs youngster Dan Smith to become Wildcats' first 2014 recruit|url=http://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/wakefield-wildcats/wakefield-wildcats-news/wakefield-wildcats-south-sydney-rabbitohs-youngster-dan-smith-to-become-wildcats-first-2014-recruit-1-6113424|website=Wakefield Express|publisher=Johnston Press|access-date=23 February 2016|date=4 October 2013|archive-date=4 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304104941/http://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/wakefield-wildcats/wakefield-wildcats-news/wakefield-wildcats-south-sydney-rabbitohs-youngster-dan-smith-to-become-wildcats-first-2014-recruit-1-6113424|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2014, he regularly appeared for Wakefield as a {{rlp|pr}}, and he scored 3 tries in 20 appearances.<ref name="loverugbyleague" /> In July 2014, Smith signed a new three-year deal with Wakefield.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/daniel-smith-extends-wakefield-stay/|title=Daniel Smith extends Wakefield stay|date=2014-07-21|website=Love Rugby League|access-date=2020-04-22}}</ref>

In 2015, he made a further 19 appearances and scored 4 tries, before leaving for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in July as part of a deal that saw [[Anthony Mullally]] move the other way.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/33523219|title=Daniel Smith: Wakefield prop forward joins Huddersfield Giants|date=2015-07-14|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-22|language=en-gb}}</ref>

==== Featherstone Rovers (dual registration) ====
In 2014, Smith played one game for [[Featherstone Rovers]] on dual registration, scoring a try in his only appearance.<ref name="loverugbyleague">{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Daniel-Smith|title=Daniel Smith Player Stats|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|access-date=2014-05-28}}</ref>

=== Huddersfield Giants ===
In July 2015, Smith was signed by [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a four-and-a-half-year deal.<ref name="huddersfield" /> 
[[File:Daniel Smith Huddersfield.jpg|thumb|right|Smith playing for the Huddersfield Giants in [[Perpignan, France]] in 2015]]
He scored his first try for the Giants against his previous club [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats|Wakefield]] just weeks after completing his move away.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/9925617/huddersfield-beat-wakefield-34-24-in-their-super-league-clash|title=Huddersfield guarantee Super League top-four spot with win over Wakefield|website=Sky Sports|language=en|access-date=2020-04-22}}</ref>

On 27 March 2019, Huddersfield announced that, as of 1 April, Smith would be released from the final year of his contract due to "him not featuring in [head coach] [[Simon Woolford|Simon [Woolford]]]'s plans".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/article/52/daniel-smith-leaves-giants|title=DANIEL SMITH LEAVES GIANTS|website=www.giantsrl.com|access-date=2020-04-22}}</ref> Across his time with Huddersfield, Smith made 50 appearances and scored 5 tries.<ref name="loverugbyleague" />

==== Oldham (loan) ====
Smith re-joined [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], this time playing in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], on loan from Huddersfield in 2017. Across July, he made 3 appearances and scored 1 try.<ref name="loverugbyleague" />

==== Featherstone Rovers (loan) ====
Smith re-joined [[Featherstone Rovers]] on a one-month loan deal in January 2019.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-forward-joins-featherstone-on-loan/|title=Huddersfield forward joins Featherstone on loan|last=Darbyshire|first=Drew|date=2019-01-29|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-22}}</ref> In his second stint at the club, he made 5 appearances and scored 2 tries.<ref name="loverugbyleague" />

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 2 April 2019, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Smith on a deal until the end of the 2020 season.<ref name="castleford">{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6312|title=Tigers Sign Smith|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-04-22}}</ref> He was assigned squad number 34. Castleford Director of Rugby [[Jon Wells]] said, "Daniel has been a target of ours for some time now and we are fortunate that the opportunity has arisen to bring him to the club earlier than we anticipated."<ref name="castleford" /> Smith himself said of the signing: "I'm from here and I've always wanted to play for this club. I hope this is my last move."<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47792999|title=Daniel Smith: Castleford Tigers sign Huddersfield Giants prop|date=2019-04-02|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-22|language=en-gb}}</ref>

Smith returned to the [[John Smiths Stadium|John Smith's Stadium]] on his first Tigers appearance, making his Castleford début against his previous club Huddersfield on 11 April 2019.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47865967|title=Super League: Huddersfield 20-18 Castleford Tigers|date=2019-04-11|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-22|language=en-gb}}</ref> He went on to appear 19 times for Castleford in 2019, scoring 1 try.<ref name="loverugbyleague" /> [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] Director of Rugby [[Jon Wells]] described him as a "hard-running, ball-playing, offloading" forward.<ref name="castleford" />

In 2020, Smith played in every game for Castleford until the season was suspended due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom|COVID-19 pandemic]]. A pectoral injury disrupted his return upon Super League's resumption in August and limited him to 10 appearances that year.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Craven|first=Dave|date=2020-12-04|title=Castleford Tigers bidding to make Daniel Smith more "durable" in 2021|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-bidding-to-make-daniel-smith-more-durable-in-2021-3058489|access-date=2022-02-08|website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> In December 2020, Smith agreed a contract extension for the 2021 season.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-12-04|title=Daniel Smith: Castleford prop agrees new contract for 2021 season|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55192228|access-date=2022-02-08|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In May 2021, Smith signed a new deal with Castleford to stay with the club for a further two years.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-05-17|title=Daniel Smith: Castleford forward signs new contract to stay with club until 2023|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57142557|access-date=2022-02-08|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite web|last=Scott|first=Ged|date=2021-07-17|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|access-date=2022-02-08|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Throughout the 2021 season, Smith made 18 appearances and scored 3 tries. He was predominantly utilised as a {{Rlp|pr}} but also covered {{Rlp|lf}} and {{Rlp|so}}.

In May 2023, it was announced that Smith would depart Castleford with immediate effect after his loan move to [[Featherstone Rovers]] was made permanent.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-05-22 |title=Smith departs the Tigers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8611 |access-date=2023-05-23 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=2023-05-20 |title=Castleford Tigers forward set for early exit with Championship move lined up |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-forward-set-early-26956670 |access-date=2023-05-23 |website=Rugby League Live}}</ref> During his time with the Tigers, he made a total 75 appearances and scored 5 tries.

==== Halifax Panthers (dual registration) ====
In February 2023, Dan Smith played one game for [[Halifax Panthers]] on dual registration from Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Heppenstall |first=Ross |date=2023-02-17 |title=Castleford Tigers prop heads to the Championship after surprise 21-man squad inclusion |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-prop-heads-championship-26268433 |access-date=2023-05-23 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref>

==== Featherstone Rovers (loan) ====
Smith returned to [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] on a two-week loan from Castleford in May 2023, marking his third spell with the Rovers.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=2023-05-03 |title=Castleford forward Daniel Smith heads to the Championship |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-forward-daniel-smith-heads-to-the-championship |access-date=2023-05-23 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He featured at {{Rlp|lf}} in wins over [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] and [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]].<ref name="featherstone">{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=2023-05-22 |title=Castleford confirm Daniel Smith exit with Championship move made permanent |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-confirm-daniel-smith-exit-with-championship-move-made-permanent |access-date=2023-05-23 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=== Featherstone Rovers ===
Following an initial loan period, Smith joined [[Featherstone Rovers]] on a permanent deal in May 2023.<ref name="featherstone" /> Head coach [[Sean Long]] said, "He's skilful, smart and a hard worker. He's a perfect fit for our brand of rugby."<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-05-22 |title=Daniel Smith is a Featherstone Player |url=https://www.featherstonerovers.co.uk/news/daniel-smith-is-a-featherstone-player-8VHsq/ |access-date=2023-05-23 |website=Featherstone Rovers}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 31 October 2023 it was reported that he would join [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] for the 2024 season on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=31 Oct 2023|title=Experienced Bradford Bulls recruit sets out Super League ambitions after becoming 27th confirmed squad member for 2024|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/experienced-bradford-bulls-recruit-sets-out-super-league-ambitions-after-becoming-27th-confirmed-squad-member-for-2024}}</ref>

On 26 Nov 2024 it was reported that he had left [[Bradford Bulls]] by mutual consent due to external work commitments.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=26 Nov 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Bradford Bulls agree to release key player from final year of his contract|url=https://www.totalrl.com/bradford-bulls-agree-to-release-key-player-from-final-year-of-his-contract/}}</ref>
